title: salix helvetica subsp. marrubiifolia
intro: Salix helvetica subsp. marrubiifolia is a subspecies of willow tree known for its unique properties. This plant is primarily used in herbal medicine due to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which gives it anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The medicinal properties of Salix helvetica subsp. marrubiifolia make it beneficial for reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various health conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and even skin conditions. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
